content.problemStatement:
"Every beginning has an end... and an editorial." - taran_1407

What the hell are all these interactive problems? What does flushing output mean? So many questions... Chef explains it in an easy way: you must communicate with a grader program, which accepts your input only if you flushed the output.

There is a contest with interactive problems where NNN people participate. Each contestant has a known rating. Chef wants to know which contestants will not forget to flush the output in interactive problems. Fortunately, he knows that contestants with rating at least rrr never forget to flush their output and contestants with rating smaller than rrr always forget to do it. Help Chef!
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers N and r. Each of the following N lines contains a single integer R denoting the rating of one contestant.
content.outputFormat: For each contestant, print a single line containing the string 'Good boi' if this contestant does not forget to flush the output or 'Bad boi' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first contestant has a rating of 1499, which is less than the threshold of 1500, so they are a 'Bad boi'. The second contestant has a rating of 1501, which is greater than or equal to the threshold, so they are a 'Good boi'.

sampleTestCases.output:
Bad boi
Good boi
Good boi
sampleTestCases.explanation: The first contestant has a rating of 1399, which is less than the threshold of 1400, so they are a 'Bad boi'. The second and third contestants have ratings of 1400 and 1401, respectively, which are greater than or equal to the threshold, so they are 'Good boi'.
The contestant has a rating of 1500, which is greater than or equal to the threshold of 1500, so they are a 'Good boi'.

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Bad boi
Good boi
Good boi

solution.approach: Read the number of contestants N and the threshold rating r. Then, for each contestant, read their rating R and check if it is greater than or equal to the threshold r. If it is, print 'Good boi', otherwise print 'Bad boi'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) — because we are iterating over each contestant once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — because we are using a constant amount of space to store the input and output.
